Claude responded: This round introduced several real bugs on top of the improvements — let me list exactly what I see before writing the fix list:

Identified multiple UI bugs: text overflow, spacing issues, icon inconsistencies, color mismatches

This round introduced several real bugs on top of the improvements — let me list exactly what I see before writing the fix list:

  1. Broken text animation: hero subhead cuts off mid-word — "Build a resume tailored for a D" — the typewriter/rotating-text effect is stuck mid-cycle.
  2. Orphaned stray character: a lone "-" sits alone in a large empty gap between the feature grid and the live demo section.
  3. Missing section heading: "LIVE DEMONSTRATION" eyebrow + "Watch AI optimize a bullet in real time" heading are gone entirely — the live demo card now has no heading above it.
  4. Score mismatch bug: the badge says "ATS SCORE: 35%" but the ring next to it shows "10" — two different numbers for the same score.
  5. Overlapping text: the closing CTA heading "Ready to build a resume that actually gets read?" is rendering behind/overlapped by something else above it.
  6. Lost the teal headline accent: "the algorithms" is no longer colored — it's back to plain black, losing the gradient highlight established earlier.
  7. Icon regressions persist: AI Resume Agent (purple) and Real-Time Sync (blue) still haven't been fixed to teal, and LaTeX Compilation now uses a plain grey circle icon while its neighbors use colored square badges — inconsistent again.
Created a file, read a file

Worth flagging the pattern across the last several rounds: items 6–8 are repeat regressions — things that were fixed once, then broke again in a later pass. That usually means the tool is regenerating larger chunks of the page each time rather than making targeted edits, so earlier fixes get silently overwritten. If this keeps happening after this bug-fix pass, it's worth explicitly telling the tool to make surgical edits to the existing code rather than regenerating sections wholesale — that's a different instruction than anything about design, but it might be the actual root cause at this point.

Ats resify bugfix punchlist

Claude responded: Good news on the bugs — nearly everything from the last punch list is fixed (score sync, missing heading, orphaned dash, CTA overlap, "the algorithms" gradient…

Diagnosed grid spacing issues, advocated carousel implementation instead

Good news on the bugs — nearly everything from the last punch list is fixed (score sync, missing heading, orphaned dash, CTA overlap, "the algorithms" gradient). Two small leftovers I still see: the hero subhead cuts off "Netfli" (truncated "Netflix" — same typewriter-timing bug, not fully fixed), and the "LaTeX Compilation" icon is still a plain outline document icon while its neighbors use filled teal badges.

But you're right that the feature grid itself is the real problem now — even with spacing patched, it's had uneven card heights across four separate rounds. At this point, patching the grid again is probably not going to fix it. Since you already have the FocusRail carousel adapted to your color system, I'd switch to that now instead of continuing to fight the grid. Here's the consolidated, ready-to-send implementation prompt:

Created a file, read a file

One thing worth your attention that isn't purely technical: the rotating hero text is now naming real companies (Stripe, Netflix) as example job targets. I flagged it as a judgment call rather than forcing a change, but it's worth deciding deliberately — using real, recognizable company logos/names in marketing copy without any affiliation can look like an implied partnership claim, which is the kind of thing that draws unwanted legal attention once a site starts getting real traffic. Easy fix either way (generic role framing, or a disclaimer), just flagging it now while it's cheap to change rather than after launch.

Created a file, read a file

I gave the truncation bug real weight this time — three failures in a row on the same element means it's worth giving the tool an explicit fallback (ship a static phrase) rather than asking for a fourth attempt at the same animation. If it comes back broken again after this, I'd stop asking for the fix and just tell it to remove the rotation outright — a boring, correct subhead beats an impressive, broken one.

Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.


Ats resify hero panel refinement · MD

Refine Hero Code Panels (experience_input.txt / preview_compiled.pdf)

Bug — specific model name still present

The status line under the left panel reads "Gemini 2.0 active." This violates the standing rule established earlier in this project: do not name specific LLM models anywhere in the UI copy. Replace with a generic status label instead — e.g. "AI Enhancement active," "Model active," or simply drop the model reference and keep only the metric ("42% latency cut · 0 deadlocks"). Search the rest of the page for any other instance of a named model version and remove those too.

Missing glassmorphism on these specific panels

Both the left (input/output) and right (resume preview) panels currently render as flat, solid white cards with a hard border and drop shadow — there's no visible blur or translucency, which breaks consistency with the glass treatment already established on the feature cards and live-demo card elsewhere on the page. Apply the same glass treatment here:

  • Background: rgba(255,255,255,0.6) (not solid #FFFFFF)
  • backdrop-filter: blur(20px)
  • Border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.7) instead of a solid gray/black border
  • Soft, large-radius, low-opacity shadow instead of the current harder-edged shadow
  • This should be visually obvious against the page's teal-tinted background blobs — if there's nothing to blur, add a very subtle background gradient/blob behind the hero (consistent with the ambient glow already used elsewhere) so the glass effect has something to catch

Polish pass on panel internals

  • The inner "INPUT" and "LATEX OUTPUT" sub-blocks (pink/red-tinted and teal-tinted respectively) should also pick up a light glass treatment — slightly translucent tint over the blurred glass parent, rather than a flat solid pastel background, so they feel like they belong to the same glass system rather than sitting as opaque colored boxes on top of it
  • Confirm consistent corner radius across the outer panel, the inner input/output sub-blocks, and the buttons below — right now the outer panel, inner blocks, and pill-shaped buttons all use different radius values; pick one scale (e.g. 12px for inner blocks, 20px for the outer panel, fully rounded for pill buttons) and apply deliberately rather than inconsistently
  • The score ring (98) and "LIVE" indicator on the right panel are working well — keep those as-is, no changes needed there

Bar for done

Compare these two panels side by side with the feature cards and live-demo card elsewhere on the page. They should look like they're made of the same glass material — same blur intensity, same border treatment, same shadow softness. Right now the hero panels look like an earlier, flatter design pass that didn't get the glassmorphism update the rest of the page received.
